title: Independent external WORM terminal-receipt readback ACK (S1.2A)
description: Canonical self-hashed readback acknowledgement produced by a DISTINCT verifier (readback_verifier_id must differ from the committed result's append_actor_id). Against the INJECTED S3-compatible client the verifier re-reads the object (get_object/head_object + get_object_retention), asserting checksum_match + version_id_match + retention_match (the retention mode matches AND retain_until is in the FUTURE), and proves immutability NON-DESTRUCTIVELY via object_lock_enabled (get_object_lock_configuration shows the bucket Object-Lock ENABLED). No destructive delete/put_object_retention probe is ever performed (a misconfigured bucket would otherwise DELETE the just-committed record). A positive ack (ack=true) requires a distinct verifier, same_actor_violation=false, and immutability_proven=true (checksum_match AND version_id_match AND retention_match AND object_lock_enabled). The append actor reading its own record must set same_actor_violation=true and refuse to ack. These distinctness/immutability rules are enforced in the delegated Python validator.
